title: Sersalisia sessiliflora
intro: Sersalisia sessiliflora, commonly known as the native cherry or native plum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and relieve digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Sersalisia sessiliflora include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Sersalisia sessiliflora is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
